title: Little Pear Strudels
excerpt: Ingredients 50 g butter 2 x 410 g cans of preserved pears, drained and cut into small cubes 30 ml sugar 30 ml honey (or to taste) 65 ml seedless sultanas 4 crumbled Tennis or Marie biscuits 6 sheets of filo pastry about 45 ml melted butter Method In a pan, heat the 50 …
html:
Ingredients

  • 50 g butter
  • 2 x 410 g cans of preserved pears, drained and cut into small cubes
  • 30 ml sugar
  • 30 ml honey (or to taste)
  • 65 ml seedless sultanas
  • 4 crumbled Tennis or Marie biscuits
  • 6 sheets of filo pastry
  • about 45 ml melted butter

Method

In a pan, heat the 50 g butter and add the pears, honey and sultanas and cook briskly without a lid for 5 – 8 minutes until the sauce has reduced and thickened.

Remove the pan from the heat, stir in the biscuit crumbs and set the pan aside so the mixture can cool.

Brush one sheet of filo pastry with melted butter and place a second sheet of pastry on top.

Cut the sheets down the middle so you have two pieces of double-layered pastry.

Divide the pear mixture into six equal portions and now spoon a portion onto the long edge of each double-layered piece of pastry.

Fold the sides of the pastry in slightly and then roll up each little parcel of pastry to look like a fat cigar.

Brush another layer of melted butter over the strudels and pack them onto a baking tray with the seams underneath.

Cut about 2 – 3 small slits into the top of the strudel.

Now make another 4 similar strudels and bake at 200°C for 10 – 15 minutes or until the pastry is crisp and golden brown.

Serve 6 portions with whipped cream or ice cream.

Easy!

Tip: You can use any tinned fruit; even fresh mango. Just make sure the mixture is well drained. Pears and mango can also be flavoured by adding about 45 ml finely chopped, glazed ginger and then using ginger syrup instead of honey.
